pages.full_text:
more sun. Small zigzag roads to Seapalling.

	Lots of ex-windmills.

We found a pleasant small campsite on The old bridge road in Potter Heigham. After opening the campingvan curtains in the afternoon we saw a group of Dutch campers who had come on a course about making rammed earth houses.

	In the evening we visited a boat yard on the river Yare. A company Sheerline who make river boats took a chad catalogue. We also went to a rural business park that was well designed and dropped catalogues at two interior design firms. It was then an hour to Halesworth, but we stopped just north at Spexhall and walked through the churchyard, across a field and stream up to a farm and back. Tonic showed a return of youthful energy. After lunch we picked up lampshades from WK Howarth and set off for Annette, briefly stopping to drop another catalogue on the way.

	Annette gave us tea in the garden in pleasant sunshine. We returned to 86 at about 7.45. Charles had already eate.
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence, continuing from previous page. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. 'eate' at end appears to be a cut-off word (likely 'eaten'). 'Annette' appears to be a person's name, not a place. '86' likely refers to a house number. The opening text in green ink ('more sun. Small zigzag roads to Seapalling. Lots of ex-windmills.') suggests a different pen/ink from the rest.

pages.full_text:
Mayors Sunday
At 9.50, we went to church & then on to Enville for lunch in a field. (S.E, Tonic)
	At 3.45 we arrived at Pat Gregory's. Her sister, Barbara, visited too from 5 doors up the road. Lovely hills visible from the house, Caer Caradoc, Church Stretton. 3 kinds of cake! Beautiful stream & garden.

	On to Hamperley campsite, SE corner Long Mynd. We walked before bed. It was warm in the sun. 2 families had b.b.q.s. Log fire in the woods. Games — football & frisbee in the field. Large flock of sheep.

	Rain in the morning & we got ~~stuck~~ stuck again! 15 years on from the first time. 6 Sea Cadets from Kiddel & Kingswi[?] came straight over, 5 men & one woman, & pushed us out. We had breakfast by the gate.

	On to Oswestry by a route to the west partly in Wales
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '6' visible in top-right corner. 'Mayors Sunday' is likely a local event name rather than a date, but noted in dates as uncertain. 'Hamperley' campsite — could also read as 'Hamperley' or 'Hamperley'; SE corner of Long Mynd in Shropshire. 'Kingswi[?]' is partially legible — could be 'Kingswi[nford]' or similar. The strikethrough on the first 'stuck' is in the original text. 'Pat Gregory's' is a person's home, not a campsite. '(S.E, Tonic)' likely refers to people's initials. Page text cuts off mid-sentence at bottom.
